source_docs.title: Optimized single-nucleus transcriptional profiling by combinatorial indexing
The single-cell transcriptional landscape of mammalian organogenesis
The single-cell transcriptional landscape of mammalian organogenesis

oligos.name: Indexed oligo-dT RT primer
oligos.role: Barcoded reverse-transcription primer; introduces index 1 (RT barcode) and the UMI, and primes first-strand cDNA synthesis on the polyA tail

components.name: Linker (CAGAGC)
components.role: 6-bp constant linker; template for ligation-primer annealing

oligos.notes: From the optimized protocol: 5'-/5Phos/CAGAGCNNNNNNNN[10bp RT index]TTTT...(30 T)-3'. The 8 N's are the UMI; CAGAGC is the 6-bp linker used for the downstream ligation. Ordered as 96-384 indexed primers.

oligos.name: Indexed ligation (hairpin) primer
oligos.role: Hairpin ligation adaptor; introduces index 2 (ligation barcode) and the Read 1 primer region, ligated to the phosphorylated 5' end of the RT primer

components.name: Anti-linker splint (GCTCTG)
components.role: reverse complement of CAGAGC; anneals to RT-primer linker

components.name: Ligation barcode (reverse complement)
components.role: hairpin stem (reverse complement of ligation barcode)

oligos.notes: 5'-GCTCTG[9- or 10-bp ligation index]TACGACGCTCTTCCGATCT[reverse complement of ligation index]-3'. GCTCTG is the reverse complement of the CAGAGC linker and anneals to it; the 3' copy of the barcode is its reverse complement and folds back to form the hairpin stem. The original sci-RNA-seq3 (Cao 2019) placed a /dideoxyU/ at the T before ACGACG and required a USER step; the optimized protocol removed the deoxyU and the USER step.

oligos.name: Indexed PCR P7 primer
oligos.role: Library PCR primer; introduces index 3 (i7 PCR barcode) and the P7 flow-cell adaptor on the Tn5/Nextera side

components.name: Nextera N7 (partial)
components.role: anneals to the N7 tagmentation adaptor

oligos.notes: 5'-CAAGCAGAAGACGGCATACGAGAT[PCR P7 index]GTCTCGTGGGCTCGG-3'. 96-384 indexed primers. GTCTCGTGGGCTCGG is the 5' portion of the Tn5-N7 adaptor.

oligos.name: PCR P5 (TruSeq) primer
oligos.role: Library PCR primer; adds the P5 flow-cell adaptor, an optional i5 plate index, and the full TruSeq Read 1 sequence on the RT/ligation side
oligos.notes: 5'-AATGATACGGCGACCACCGAGATCTACAC[PCR P5 index]ACACTCTTTCCCTACACGACGCTCTTCCGATCT-3'. The P5 index is optional: a single P5 primer suffices for one plate of PCR, or a full 96-384 set enables fully dual-indexed PCR / plate indexing.

oligos.name: Tn5-N7 oligo
oligos.role: Non-transferred strand of the N7 transposome; carries the Nextera N7 / Read 2 primer region and the mosaic end
oligos.notes: 5'-GTCTCGTGGGCTCGGAGATGTGTATAAGAGACAG-3' (high-purity salt-free). Annealed 1:1 with the ME oligo and loaded onto unloaded Tn5.

oligos.name: Mosaic End (ME) oligo
oligos.role: Transferred (phosphorylated) strand of the N7 transposome; the ME sequence appended to fragmented cDNA during tagmentation
oligos.notes: 5'-/5Phos/CTGTCTCTTATACACATCT-3' (high-purity salt-free). Reverse complement of the AGATGTGTATAAGAGACAG mosaic end.

oligos.name: Annealed N7 Tn5 adaptor
oligos.role: Double-stranded transposon adaptor loaded onto Tn5 for tagmentation of the double-stranded cDNA
oligos.notes: Tn5-N7 and ME resuspended to 100 uM in annealing buffer (50 mM NaCl, 40 mM Tris-HCl pH 8.0), mixed 1:1 to a 50 uM working stock, and annealed (95 C 5 min, cool to 65 C, 65 C 5 min, cool to 4 C).

oligos.name: Illumina TruSeq Read 1 primer
oligos.role: Sequencing primer for Read 1 (cell barcode + UMI); standard Illumina primer matching the P5-side of the library
oligos.notes: Standard Illumina TruSeq Read 1 primer; its sequence is built into the library by the PCR P5 primer. Read 1 = 34 cycles.

oligos.name: Illumina Nextera Read 2 primer
oligos.role: Sequencing primer for Read 2 (cDNA insert); standard Illumina Nextera primer matching the P7/N7 side of the library
oligos.notes: Standard Illumina Nextera/N7 Read 2 primer; sequence built into the library by the Tn5-N7 adaptor. Read 2 = 48 cycles (optimized) / 52 cycles (original 2019).

oligos.name: Illumina P5 adapter
oligos.role: P5 flow-cell binding adaptor (5' end of the final library)
oligos.notes: Introduced by the PCR P5 (TruSeq) primer.

oligos.name: Illumina P7 adapter
oligos.role: P7 flow-cell binding adaptor (3' end of the final library)
oligos.notes: Introduced by the PCR P7 primer; appears as its reverse complement (ATCTCGTATGCCGTCTTCTGCTTG) at the 3' end of the top strand.

annotation_lines: [LIGATION_BARCODE:10] = Ligation barcode (index 2, part of cell barcode; read as reverse complement)
[RT_BARCODE:10] = RT barcode (index 1, part of cell barcode)
CCGAGCCCACGAGAC = Nextera N7 (reverse complement of GTCTCGTGGGCTCGG)

library_generation.title: Indexed reverse transcription (index 1)
library_generation.summary: Barcoded oligo-dT RT primers capture polyadenylated mRNA in fixed nuclei and make first-strand cDNA, adding the RT barcode and UMI.
library_generation.note: Superscript IV reverse transcriptase extends the anchored oligo-dT primer along the mRNA. The primer carries a 5' phosphate, the CAGAGC linker, an 8-bp UMI, a 10-bp well-specific RT barcode, and a 30-nt poly(dT). Performed in-well across a 96-/384-well plate so nuclei from different samples get distinct RT barcodes.
library_generation.product:
(1) First-strand cDNA after indexed RT:

5'- /5Phos/CAGAGC[UMI:8][RT_BARCODE:10]TTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTT[CDNA]--------> -3'
                                       ||||||||||
                                   3'- AAAAAAAAAA......[mRNA] -5'
(mRNA template is nicked and used to prime second-strand synthesis in step 3)

library_generation.title: Hairpin ligation (index 2)
library_generation.summary: After pooling and re-splitting, a hairpin ligation primer anneals to the CAGAGC linker and T4 DNA ligase joins the ligation barcode onto the 5' end of the RT primer.
library_generation.note: The ligation primer's GCTCTG overhang is the reverse complement of the CAGAGC linker; its 3' arm folds back (barcode / reverse-complement barcode) into a hairpin, presenting a recessed 3'-OH that is ligated to the phosphorylated 5' end of the RT primer. This adds index 2 and the Read 1 primer sequence.
library_generation.product:
(2) After ligation of the hairpin barcode adaptor:

5'- GCTCTG[LIGATION_BARCODE:10]TACGACGCTCTTCCGATCT[LIGATION_BARCODE:10]CAGAGC[UMI:8][RT_BARCODE:10](T)30[CDNA] -3'
     ^GCTCTG anneals to the CAGAGC linker; T4 DNA ligase seals the 5'-phosphate;
      the 3' [LIGATION_BARCODE:10] is the reverse complement forming the hairpin stem

library_generation.title: Second-strand synthesis
library_generation.summary: The RNA is nicked and used to prime synthesis of the second strand, opening the hairpin and yielding double-stranded cDNA.
library_generation.note: NEBNext mRNA second-strand synthesis module (DNA Polymerase I) performs RNA-primed nick-translation, converting the barcoded first-strand product into blunt double-stranded DNA and resolving the ligation hairpin.

library_generation.title: Protease digestion + Tn5 (N7) tagmentation
library_generation.summary: Nuclei are digested with a heat-inactivatable protease, then N7-loaded Tn5 fragments the double-stranded cDNA and inserts the Nextera N7 adaptor.
library_generation.note: Qiagen protease frees the DNA for Tn5 access (replacing the original bead cleanup). Tn5 loaded only with the N7 (mosaic-end) adaptor tagments the dsDNA, appending the mosaic end and Read 2 / N7 sequence. Only the read-1-proximal fragment that retains the cell barcodes is amplifiable in the next step.

library_generation.title: Indexed PCR (index 3) and final adaptors
library_generation.summary: PCR with an indexed P7 primer and a (optionally indexed) P5/TruSeq primer adds index 3, the full TruSeq Read 1, and the P5 and P7 flow-cell adaptors, completing the sequenceable library.
library_generation.note: The P5 primer anneals to the Read 1 region and adds P5 + optional i5 + full TruSeq Read 1; the P7 primer anneals to the N7 region and adds the i7 barcode + P7. 16 cycles with a 70 C pre-extension. Library is gel size-selected to ~250-600 bp.

library_sequencing.note: Reads the cell barcode + UMI: 10-bp ligation barcode + CAGAGC linker (6) + 8-bp UMI + 10-bp RT barcode = 34 bp. Primer is identical to the top-strand TruSeq R1 region, anneals to the bottom strand, and reads rightward.
library_sequencing.diagram:
Read 1 (34 cycles) - Illumina TruSeq Read 1 primer:

   Read 1 primer 5'-ACACTCTTTCCCTACACGACGCTCTTCCGATCT------------------------------------>
5'-...ACACTCTTTCCCTACACGACGCTCTTCCGATCTNNNNNNNNNNCAGAGCNNNNNNNNNNNNNNNNNNTTTTTTTTTT...-3'
3'-...TGTGAGAAAGGGATGTGCTGCGAGAAGGCTAGANNNNNNNNNNGTCTCGNNNNNNNNNNNNNNNNNNAAAAAAAAAA...-5'
                                       |-Lig BC(10)-|link||-UMI(8)-||- RT BC(10)-|

library_sequencing.note: Reads into the cDNA insert from the Tn5/N7 (P7) side. Primer anneals to the top-strand ME/N7 region and extends leftward into the cDNA. 48 cycles (optimized); 52 in the original 2019 protocol.
library_sequencing.diagram:
Read 2 (48 cycles) - Illumina Nextera Read 2 primer:

 Read 2 primer 3'-GACAGAGAATATGTGTAGAGGCTCGGGTGCTCTG-5'
              <-------------------------------------
5'-...XXXXXXXCTGTCTCTTATACACATCTCCGAGCCCACGAGACNNNNNNNNNNATCTCGTATGCC...-3'
3'-...XXXXXXXGACAGAGAATATGTGTAGAGGCTCGGGTGCTCTGNNNNNNNNNNTAGAGCATACGG...-5'
      |-cDNA-||------ME + N7 (rc) junction------||-i7(10)-|

library_sequencing.note: Reads index 3 / the PCR P7 barcode (10 bp). Primer is identical to the top-strand ME/N7 region, anneals to the bottom strand, and reads rightward into the i7 index.
library_sequencing.diagram:
Index read 1 / i7 (10 cycles) - Nextera Index 1 primer:

   i7 primer 5'-CTGTCTCTTATACACATCTCCGAGCCCACGAGAC---------->
5'-...CTGTCTCTTATACACATCTCCGAGCCCACGAGACNNNNNNNNNNATCTCGTATGCC...-3'
3'-...GACAGAGAATATGTGTAGAGGCTCGGGTGCTCTGNNNNNNNNNNTAGAGCATACGG...-5'
                                        |- i7 (10)-|

library_sequencing.note: Optional PCR P5 / plate index (10 bp), present only when the P5 primer is indexed. On the NextSeq/NovaSeq platforms used here it is read as the reverse complement: the primer anneals to the top-strand TruSeq R1 region and extends leftward into the i5 index.
library_sequencing.diagram:
Index read 2 / i5 (10 cycles, optional plate index) - read as reverse complement on NextSeq/NovaSeq:

                             i5 primer 3'-TGTGAGAAAGGGATGTGCTGCGAGAAGGCTAGA-5'
                                     <-------------------------------------
5'-AATGATACGGCGACCACCGAGATCTACACNNNNNNNNNNACACTCTTTCCCTACACGACGCTCTTCCGATCT...-3'
3'-TTACTATGCCGCTGGTGGCTCTAGATGTGNNNNNNNNNNTGTGAGAAAGGGATGTGCTGCGAGAAGGCTAGA...-5'
   |------- P5 --------------||-i5(10)-||-------- TruSeq Read 1 ---------|

title: sci-RNA-seq3
description: sci-RNA-seq3 is a three-level single-cell combinatorial-indexing RNA-seq method that profiles the transcriptomes of hundreds of thousands to millions of single nuclei in one experiment, without droplets or a commercial kit. Fixed nuclei are split across 96-/384-well plates and labeled by three successive in-well barcoding reactions: a barcoded oligo-dT reverse-transcription primer that also carries a UMI (index 1), a ligated hairpin barcode (index 2), and an indexed PCR (index 3), so each nucleus receives a unique RT + ligation + PCR barcode combination. After second-strand synthesis and Tn5 (N7) tagmentation, the hybrid TruSeq(P5)/Nextera(P7) library is sequenced on Illumina: Read 1 recovers the ligation barcode, UMI and RT barcode (together the cell barcode), Read 2 reads the cDNA insert, and the i7/i5 index reads recover the PCR barcodes.

reference.label: Optimized single-nucleus transcriptional profiling by combinatorial indexing

original_publication.title: Optimized single-nucleus transcriptional profiling by combinatorial indexing

throughput.summary: A one-plate 96x96x96 experiment typically nets ~100,000 nuclei; scaling to 96x96x384 gives ~400,000 and a four-plate 384x384x384 experiment yields one to several million nuclei per run. Reagent cost is on the order of 1 cent per cell or less.
throughput.cells: ~100,000 (96x96x96) up to >1,000,000 (384x384x384); showcase E16.5 mouse embryo run recovered 381,888 high-quality nuclei (median 2,648 UMIs and 1,493 genes per cell)
throughput.rna: single-nucleus mRNA (3' UMI counting); nuclear RNA, ~59% intronic
throughput.dna: not applicable (RNA assay; the cDNA library is the sequenced product)

publication.statistical_model: Negative binomial generalized linear model for UMI counts (Monocle differentialGeneTest / spline fits); Poisson-like combinatorial-indexing collision model; Scrublet for doublet detection

other.label: sci-RNA-seq3 original publication
other.value: Cao, J. et al. The single-cell transcriptional landscape of mammalian organogenesis. Nature 566, 496-502 (2019). doi:10.1038/s41586-019-0969-x (MOCA: ~2 million nuclei from 61 mouse embryos, E9.5-E13.5)

other.label: sci-RNA-seq original method
other.value: Cao, J. et al. Comprehensive single-cell transcriptional profiling of a multicellular organism. Science 357, 661-667 (2017)

other.label: Barcode combinatorial capacity
other.value: 384 RT x 384 ligation x 768 PCR barcodes = ~113 million possible combinations (Cao 2019)

other.label: Key optimizations vs. original
other.value: DEPC RNase inactivation, DSP/methanol fixation, T4 DNA ligase (not Quick Ligase), protease digestion instead of bead cleanup, and removal of the deoxyU / USER step

other.label: Analysis pipeline
other.value: https://github.com/JunyueC/sci-RNA-seq3_pipeline (demultiplex on PCR i5/i7, filter RT + ligation indexes, STAR alignment, UMI dedup)

other.label: Data availability
other.value: GSE186824 (optimized E16.5 mouse embryo); GSE119945 (original MOCA atlas)

other.label: Sequencing platforms
other.value: NextSeq 550 / NextSeq 2000 P2 for one-plate runs; NovaSeq 6000 S4 for large (four-plate) experiments

data_processing.summary: A custom, non-droplet pipeline (JunyueC/sci-RNA-seq3_pipeline) converts base calls to FASTQ, demultiplexes on the PCR i5/i7 indexes, attaches and filters the in-well RT and ligation barcodes, aligns to the genome with STAR, splits reads into single cells by the RT + ligation index, and collapses UMIs to build a cell-by-gene digital expression matrix. It is qsub/HPC-oriented and produces large (3-4x) intermediate files.
